title: lathyrus lycicus subsp. phaselitanus
intro: Lathyrus lycicus subsp. phaselitanus, also known as Phaseoloid Lathyrus or Phaselitanus, is a subspecies of the Lathyrus lycicus plant, which is a type of wild pea or grass pea. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties. The medicinal properties of Lathyrus lycicus subsp. phaselitanus have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of menopause, and providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
